name: Storage and Data
summary: Schemas, SQL, file-backed storage posture, APFS/FileVault/Secure Enclave doctrine, lawfulContext, direct-identifier, and reidentification boundaries.
invariants: Filesystem-as-record posture remains primary; SQL/object layers do not replace Core storage law.
lawfulContext is required for governed storage access.
Direct identifiers and reidentification remain tightly governed.
APFS, FileVault, and Secure Enclave posture reduce substrate trust but do not replace Core law.
allowedWork: Maintain JSON Schemas, SQL shape, and Swift storage contracts.
Update mirrors together when shared storage/data contracts change.
Maintain lawfulContext, direct-identifier, and reidentification checks.
Record storage and restore gaps honestly.
forbiddenWork: Treat SQL or object backend as a replacement for filesystem-as-record governance.
Treat backup existence as proof of restore readiness.
knownGaps: Production backup, restore, retention, and DR automation remain future hardening.
SQL-backed async executor remains blocked/future work.
notes: Storage/data work commonly touches cross-language contracts and must avoid partial mirror updates.
Territory metadata does not grant storage access.
